title: 1.1: Reports unique keywords loaded into the Aho-Corasick trie (e.g., 914 unique keywords).
1.2: Reports keywords shared by multiple detectors (e.g., 32 keywords shared across detectors such as 'azure' or 'github').
1.3: States decoding happens before keyword matching (e.g., decoder.FromChunk processes chunks first, then FindDetectorMatches runs on decoded data).

1.4: Reports a verbose output sequence for chunks with both plain and encoded secrets (e.g., UTF8 decoder finding plaintext showing 'Decoder Type: PLAIN', then Base64 decoder finding encoded showing 'Decoder Type: BASE64').
1.5: Reports verification cache metrics at scan completion (e.g., Hits, Misses, HitsWasted, AttemptsSaved, VerificationTimeSpentMS fields).
1.6: Reports hit and miss numbers remain unchanged on consecutive scans (e.g., both first and second scans show Misses: 1, Hits: 0).
1.7: States worker multipliers applied (e.g., detector workers use 8x multiplier, scanner/verificationOverlap/notifier workers use 1x multiplier).
1.8: States whether observable queuing or backpressure occurs during chunk processing (e.g., buffered channels absorb bursts, or scanner workers wait for detector workers).
1.9: Reports deduplication cache key format (e.g., detector type + raw secret + RawV2 + source metadata concatenated).
1.10: Reports only detectors that found results are tracked in average detector timing (e.g., condition requires len(results) > 0 before recording timing).

1.11: Reports average detector timing includes verification time showing an increase when verification is enabled (e.g., timing increases (100-1000x or more) from microseconds to milliseconds when verification is enabled).

1.12: Reports whether same secret found via different decoders (plaintext vs base64) gets deduplicated or reported multiple times (e.g., explains that deduplication depends on SourceMetadata - same file/line = deduplicated, different files = both reported).
